summary: Google has added a backlink viewing tool through its Sitemaps/Webmaster Console, allowing site owners to see who links to their website. McKenzie finds this useful for tracking the SEO impact of his marketing efforts. He notes that most of his backlinks come from download sites or spam blogs exploiting bingo-related keywords to boost casino sites. However, he finds some genuine links from real customers and useful content, including an elementary school linking to his Dolch sight word list page.
key_points: Google's Webmaster Console now lets site owners view their backlinks, previously opaque data
Backlinks are critical for SEO and can reveal whether marketing efforts are working
Most backlinks for small software sites come from low-quality sources like download directories and spam blogs
Creating genuinely useful content (like the Dolch sight word list) earns organic backlinks from real users
money_quotes: This is useful as backlinks are SEO mana from heaven and you can spy on whether your marketing is having any effect.
The overwhelming majority are either download sites or spam blogs.
They search for bingo related keywords and link to them with the hope of getting Google to rank their casino site — I hate this.
The Dolch sight word list, for example, got linked to by an elementary school.
key_insight: Backlink data reveals both the spam ecosystem gaming search engines and the genuine organic links earned by creating useful content for your target audience.
